title: Fan analysis of 2xx themes of purpose and dependency
display_title: Fan analysis of 2xx themes of purpose and dependency
wikitext:
[]: Fan analysis of 2.xx: themes of purpose and dependency             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat May 21, 2011 11:48 am The video linked to below raises some pretty interesting points about 2.xx. Some food for thought and for further debate/analysis, perhaps? [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a-FcImzZ22A[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat May 21, 2011 11:58 am I'll check it out tonight probably but just one question: This wasn't put together by one of you-know-who's "people", was it? (I'm sure they have valid ideas on Eva things but he himself is....yeaaah.) Thanks for the link, I personally like hearing different perspectives on the new films.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 21, 2011 12:46 pm It#s always interesting - I wouldn't agree to the puppet angle, Shinji developed some rebellious spirit by the ep 4 equivalent. The "You sometimes need to work with ppl you don't like" thing is a point. I think the central theme is more one of change and growing up, but it was interesting to see it from another angle. It IS meant to make you come zup with your own ideas, after all. That Mari, while fitting neatly into our established bunch of loners and clearly prefering to work alone (The line in the beginning, she cuts off the intercom, she hardly interacts with anyone...) has no problem with Teamwork when there is no other way (Whatever organisations she's cooperating with, the stunt with Rei...) is an important point about her. I always saw the introduction battles as means of contrasts, but I saw those contrasts mainly in their battle styles - I hadn't even thought about evaluating the way they portray it. But this guy is right - Mari always blurts out what she thinks, so we see her do so. Asuka mostly puts up a mask of coolness, so we see her from the outside first. As for what happened to Asuka, I don't think it was intended as punishment of any sorts - It was probably just some drama/ thje setback/life-vbeing-a-bitch that occassionally happens in real life, a hardship to test the characters, to test whether Shinji had already found his motivations and grown up (He hadn't, not yet. But he improves later.) and to have Rei prove her unconditional luv (He's an angsty kid that ocassionally threatens to leave. Still willing to blow yourself up to him? She was.) Asuka's probably being tested as well - when she wakes up, her reactions to whatever happened to her will show if her change in attitude is genuine.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun May 22, 2011 6:19 am I now wish Funi had cast someone with this guy's accent as the English Gendo... who would be, inexplicably, the only person who has it. As for Anno "punishing" Asuka, that's not the case at all. From another thread (which would drive this guy crazy ): Warren Peace wrote: One way of creating drama is to take a character in high spirits down to the depths of depression (and vice versa). That's all the film does. Anno isn't painting Asuka as an asshole who dares to dream of a better life. He knows it means more if she suffers disaster in a moment of triumph rather than in a moment of, say, chewing gum.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun May 22, 2011 8:51 am And anyways, its a basic way of writing good stories. You take the main characters, and you have horrible things done to them (a la Romeo and Juliet, etc.). This is why it is debatable whether or not that Asuka is Anno's favorite character .

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sun May 22, 2011 9:22 am Stryker wrote: And anyways, its a basic way of writing good stories. You take the main characters, and you have horrible things done to them (a la Romeo and Juliet, etc.). This is why it is debatable whether or not that Asuka is Anno's favorite character .

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun May 22, 2011 9:23 am the_seventh_child wrote: A way of writing a good story is to constantly shit on the main character? Well, that's a new one.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun May 22, 2011 9:31 am the_seventh_child wrote: A way of writing a good story is to constantly shit on the main character? Well, that's a new one.

[]:             Quote Post by Knuckles » Mon May 23, 2011 7:05 pm I personally enjoyed the Digging Deeper video linked in the OP's video's description better.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 23, 2011 7:24 pm Stryker wrote: This is why it is debatable whether or not that Asuka is Anno's favorite character .

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon May 23, 2011 7:31 pm Reichu wrote: Making characters suffer is a good way to develop them, therefore Asuka might not be Anno's favorite? If this is what you're saying, I don't follow.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 23, 2011 7:39 pm I'm pretty sure that Asuka got all of those things back in NGE. I'm not touching the Soryu vs. Shiki thing, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon May 23, 2011 7:42 pm Reichu wrote: I'm pretty sure that Asuka got all of those things back in NGE. I'm not touching the Soryu vs. Shiki thing, though.

[]:             Quote Post by SpacemanSchmuck » Tue May 24, 2011 12:14 pm Kendrix wrote: As for what happened to Asuka, I don't think it was intended as punishment of any sorts -
